decisions.reasoning: The game starts with placing settlements. We should place settlements on nodes that produce resources frequently. The node (0, 0, 0) produces BRICK on 3 (2 pips) and ORE on 5 (4 pips). This is a strategic choice as both resources are needed for building. No other node has as many pips, making this the best initial move.

The game just started, and I need to expand my network. Building a road from my settlement at node 19 will allow me to connect to new intersections and potentially claim more resources in the future. Node 20 is a good choice as it is adjacent and will help me expand.

As the first player, I should focus on securing resources and building a strong network. The BRICK 2:1 port at node 25 and the ORE 2:1 port at node 52 are attractive options. Building at node 25 would give me access to BRICKs and OREs, which are essential for future development. The distance rule prevents building too close to the opponent's settlement, so node 25 is a safe choice.
Both roads are valid, but building at (25, 26) is better because it connects to the port for brick (2:1 trade rate), which is useful for future resource acquisition.

As the first player, I should aim to establish a strong starting position by placing settlements that can produce resources and connect to the longest road. Settlements at nodes 25 and 26 are already connected by a road, so I should place a settlement at node 28 to connect to the longest road and gain access to a 3:1 port.

The robber is currently on the BRICK-producing tile (0, 0, 0), so rolling a 3 or 5 would block production. Building a road on (28, 29) or (27, 28) would connect to the existing RED settlement at 28, satisfying the distance rule. Building (28, 29) is more strategic as it connects to a port (28) for better trading opportunities.

The robber is on a brick tile, which is producing brick and wheat. Moving the robber would be beneficial. The opponent has a settlement on a brick tile (25), so moving the robber there would allow stealing a brick. No other immediate actions are necessary.
Blue needs to expand and secure resource production. Building a road from settlement@43 to node 44 is the best option as it connects to a sheep port and allows for future settlement expansion.

The opponent has a sheep and an ore in hand, which could be useful for development cards. Blue needs to expand and secure resources. Building a road to node 20 is a strategic move as it connects to an intersection with a sheep-producing tile and could help in future expansions.

The robber is currently on the BRICK producing tile (0, 0, 0), which produces on 3 and 5. Moving the robber to a WHEAT producing tile (4, 10, 11) would be beneficial. The best option is to move the robber to (0, 1, -1) to the WHEAT producing tile at (1, 10, 11) since it produces on 10 and 11, and the opponent has a settlement there.

The opponent has a city at node 25 which produces brick and ore. Moving the robber to this tile will stop production and potentially steal a resource card. The robber is currently on tile (-1, 2, -1), which is producing wood. Moving the robber to node 25 will stop the production of brick and ore, and potentially steal from the opponent.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile (3, -2, 0) and moving it would stop production. The opponent has a road and a settlement on tile 43, which is producing WHEAT and ORE. Moving the robber to that tile would steal from the opponent. Given the current VP, moving the robber is the best immediate action.
Need to expand and get resources. Building a road to connect to the settlement at 28 will allow access to a port for better trading opportunities. The BRICK 2:1 port at node 25 is a good option.

The robber is currently on the BRICK producing tile (3, 5, 11). Moving the robber to a non-producing tile will prevent the opponent from stealing resources. The most strategic move is to move the robber to the WHEAT producing tile (4, 10, 11, 12), as it produces more frequently. The tile at (1, -1, 0) is a WHEAT producing tile.

The most strategic move is to move the robber to a WHEAT-producing tile (4, 10, 11, 12) to prevent the opponent from collecting WHEAT. The tile (1, -1, 0) produces WHEAT on 11, which is a good target.

The robber is currently on a BRICK producing tile. Moving the robber away from this tile will prevent the opponent from stealing resources. The best option is to move the robber to a tile that does not produce resources for either player. The (0, 0, 0) tile is a good choice as it does not produce any resources.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile, so trading for BRICKs is beneficial. I have 3 OR, which can be traded for BRICKs at 3:1. No other resource has enough of the required quantity to make a 3:1 trade. Trading OR for WHEAT, WOOD, or SHEEP would not be as efficient.

The robber is currently on the BRICK-producing tile at (0, 0, 0). Moving the robber to a tile that produces a resource I need (WOOD, BRICK, ORE, SHEEP, WHEAT) would be beneficial. The WHEAT-producing tile at (1, 0, -1) is a good target because it produces on a 10, which is a common roll. Moving the robber there would ensure I can collect WHEAT on a 10 roll.

The opponent has a strong position with two settlements and a longest road. I need to build a settlement to increase my resource production and VP. The BRICK port at node 25 is the best option as it provides a 2:1 trade rate and is not occupied by the opponent.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile, so moving it off would be beneficial. The opponent has two brick cards, so moving the robber to a BRICK-producing tile would prevent the opponent from collecting bricks. The most optimal move is to move the robber to a BRICK-producing tile.

The robber is currently on the BRICK producing tile (0, 0, 0), which produces on 3 and 5. Moving the robber to a non-producing tile will prevent resource production and potentially allow me to build. The most strategic tile to move the robber to is (2, -2, 0) which is a WOOD producing tile on 4, 6, and 10. This will prevent resource production and is close to my settlements.

The opponent has a longer road and more VP, so I need to take actions to catch up. Building a settlement or road is the most immediate way to gain points. The opponent has a longer road and more VP. I need to gain resources to build and potentially steal VP. Playing Year of Plenty with WOOD and BRICK will give me both resources needed to build a road and a settlement.
